methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $250.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.1B - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
xAI is a Series E AI & Software company seeking valuation guidance against a comparable universe of 5 private peers (Crusoe Energy, SandboxAQ, Ayar Labs, WEKA, Modal) and 1 public comparable (PLTR). The target's last post-money valuation stands at $250.0B against total raised capital of $42.0B.

Two triangulation methods apply to this analysis. Stage-progression, anchored to the $250.0B last round valuation, applies a typical Series E-to-next-stage multiplier of 1.4x, yielding an implied value of $350.0B. Peer-median analysis across the 5 same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $3.8B, with a range spanning $1.1B (Modal, low) to $10.0B (Crusoe Energy, high).

The wide dispersion between methods reflects a structural tension: xAI's last round valuation of $250.0B sits orders of magnitude above both the peer median ($3.8B) and the peer range ceiling ($10.0B). Triangulation produces a range of $3.2B (low) to $402.5B (high), with a median of $176.9B. Material uncertainty centers on whether xAI's last-round pricing proves sustainable given comparable-company metrics, the relevance of stage-progression logic at this valuation scale, and public market sentiment (PLTR trades as the single listed reference point). A valuation substantially below the current $250.0B would signal material compression from recent round terms.
